reasoning: Andriy Govorov has held the 50m butterfly long course world record (22.27s) since July 2018. The only major long course competition of 2025, the World Aquatics Championships in Singapore, already took place in July/August, and the winner's time was only the 4th fastest overall, leaving Govorov's record intact. Furthermore, the forecast horizon (November 2025 to February 2026) strongly aligns with the short course (25m) swimming season, which typically features the Swimming World Cup in Oct/Nov and the Short Course World Championships in December. Elite swimmers do not usually peak for long course times in these winter months. Given that the record has stood firmly for 7 years and we are entering the short course season, the likelihood of anyone breaking the long course world record before February 2026 is extremely low. Probabilities slightly decrease to reflect a marginally expanding horizon and the minor possibility of an early 2026 long course meet (like a Pro Swim Series event) producing a shock result.
